title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: ares() fits Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines (MARS) models, flexible nonparametric regression technique introduced Friedman (1991). forward pass adds pairs hinge basis functions form max(0, ±(x - knot)), optionally multiplied parent term capture interactions. backward pass prunes terms minimising GCV criterion, K-fold CV criterion pmethod = "cv" nfold > 0. Beyond core MARS fitter, ares() supports hyperparameter autotuning, K-fold cross-validated pruning, bootstrap bagging, observation weights, binomial / poisson / gamma response families selected basis, residual-variance prediction intervals. vignette walks features turn.

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
code: library(roadrunner) fit <- ares(mpg ~ ., data = mtcars, nthreads = 2) print(fit) #> Call: #> ares.formula(x = mpg ~ ., data = mtcars, nthreads = 2) #>  #> MARS fit:  family = gaussian  #>   Selected terms: 6 of 21 forward-pass terms #>   RSS: 91.81  #>   GCV: 6.662  #>   Degree: 1   Penalty: 2   nthreads: 2 head(predict(fit)) #> [1] 19.36172 20.45321 25.35910 19.52265 17.68805 19.22091 head(predict(fit, mtcars[1:5, ])) #> [1] 19.36172 20.45321 25.35910 19.52265 17.68805

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: already use earth, gaussian-core fits ares() numerically close data, running multiple cores deterministically. chunk illustrates synthetic regression problem interaction quadratic component. two implementations typically agree training RSS within fraction percent, even choose slightly different knot sets.
code:
if (requireNamespace("earth", quietly = TRUE)) {   set.seed(20260509)   n <- 200; p <- 5   x <- matrix(runif(n * p), n, p)   y <- 10 * sin(pi * x[, 1] * x[, 2]) + 20 * (x[, 3] - 0.5)^2 +        10 * x[, 4] + 5 * x[, 5] + rnorm(n)   fa <- ares(x, y, degree = 2, nthreads = 2)   fe <- earth::earth(x, y, degree = 2)   cat("ares  RSS:", round(fa$rss, 3), " GCV:", round(fa$gcv, 3),       " nterms:", length(fa$selected.terms), "
")   cat("earth RSS:", round(fe$rss, 3), " GCV:", round(fe$gcv, 3),       " nterms:", length(fe$selected.terms), "
")   cat("relative RSS difference:",       round(abs(fa$rss - fe$rss) / fe$rss, 4), "
") } #> ares  RSS: 222.61  GCV: 1.707  nterms: 16  #> earth RSS: 204.18  GCV: 1.518  nterms: 15  #> relative RSS difference: 0.0903

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: core guarantee ares() varying nthreads change result. fixed seed.cv, fits bit--bit identical across thread counts, makes parallel execution safe reproduce.
code: set.seed(42) n <- 150; p <- 5 x <- matrix(runif(n * p), n, p) y <- 5 * pmax(0, x[, 1] - 0.5) * pmax(0, x[, 2] - 0.3) + rnorm(n) f1 <- ares(x, y, degree = 2, nthreads = 1) f2 <- ares(x, y, degree = 2, nthreads = 2) identical(f1$selected.terms, f2$selected.terms) #> [1] TRUE max(abs(f1$coefficients - f2$coefficients)) #> [1] 0

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: Setting autotune = TRUE runs inner K-fold cross-validated grid search (degree, penalty, nk, fast.k) refits winning combination full data. n large enough, ares() first tries grid small subsample short-circuit search one configuration wins clearly; typically cuts wall-clock time substantially accuracy loss.
code: set.seed(7) fit_at <- ares(mpg ~ ., data = mtcars, autotune = TRUE,                autotune.speed = "fast", seed.cv = 1L, nthreads = 2) fit_at$autotune$degree #> [1] 1 fit_at$autotune$penalty #> [1] 1 fit_at$autotune$nk #> [1] 42

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: binary outcomes, pass family = "binomial". Term selection runs numeric response (fast), selected basis refit binary response stats::glm.fit(). predict() returns probabilities default, linear predictor via type = "link".
code: set.seed(1) n <- 400 x <- matrix(rnorm(n * 4), n, 4) p <- plogis(0.5 * x[, 1] + 1.2 * x[, 2] * (x[, 2] > 0) - 0.4 * x[, 3]) y <- rbinom(n, 1, p) fb <- ares(x, y, family = "binomial", degree = 2, nthreads = 2) range(predict(fb)) #> [1] 0.2443712 0.9915275

title: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms
text: Fast, low-dependency machine learning algorithms R. Useful causal plug-ins (e.g., nuisance fits DML) simple predictive applications. roadrunner ships C++ backed implementations classical ML algorithms thin, base-R-style interfaces. Five core fitters today: ares() – Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines (MARS). krls() – Kernel Regularized Least Squares (KRLS). plda() – Penalized Linear Discriminant Analysis (L1 / fused-lasso). ols() – Ordinary weighted least squares HC robust SEs. logreg() – Binary logistic regression IRLS HC robust SEs. Plus meep() – cross-fitted ensemble ares() krls(), built Double Machine Learning causal-forest nuisance estimation.

title: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms
text: Low dependency. Rcpp, RcppArmadillo, RcppParallel runtime. Fast. C++ engines via Rcpp multi-core scoring via RcppParallel. Deterministic. Fits bit--bit identical across thread counts fixed seed. Familiar API. Base-R style: formula matrix interfaces standard S3 methods (predict, print, summary, plot).

title: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms
text: krls() fits Kernel Regularized Least Squares model (Hainmueller Hazlett 2014) closed-form leave-one-selection ridge penalty per-observation marginal effects. krls() mirrors KRLS::krls() numerically (fits agree ~1e-13 matched sigma lambda) 6-10x faster benchmarks n >= 500.
code: set.seed(1) n <- 200 X <- matrix(rnorm(n * 3), n, 3) y <- sin(X[, 1]) + 0.5 * X[, 2]^2 - 0.3 * X[, 3] + rnorm(n, sd = 0.2)  fit <- krls(X, y)             # default sigma = ncol(X); lambda by LOO fit$avgderivatives            # average marginal effects per variable predict(fit, X)$fit           # in-sample fitted values  # Predict on new data with pointwise SEs Xnew <- matrix(rnorm(20 * 3), 20, 3) pr <- predict(fit, Xnew, se.fit = TRUE) head(pr$fit); head(pr$se.fit)

title: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms
text: meep() cross-fits ensemble ares() krls() returns --fold predictions designed drop Double Machine Learning DoubleML causal forest grf implementations. smooth, structured signal spline + kernel ensemble fits nuisances tightly grf’s built-regression forests (--bag vs --fold R-squared toy ):
code: set.seed(1) n <- 800 X <- matrix(runif(n * 4, -2, 2), n, 4) D <- sin(X[, 1]) + 0.5 * X[, 2] + 0.3 * X[, 3]^2 + rnorm(n, sd = 0.3) Y <- D + cos(X[, 1]) + 0.4 * X[, 2]^2 + 0.5 * X[, 3] + rnorm(n, sd = 0.3)  m  <- meep(X, Y, treatment = D, folds = 5, seed = 42) m$y_hat_oof   # cross-fitted E[Y | X] m$d_hat_oof   # cross-fitted E[D | X]  # hand the cross-fitted nuisances to a causal forest # grf::causal_forest(X, Y, D, Y.hat = m$y_hat_oof, W.hat = m$d_hat_oof) cf <- grf::causal_forest(X, Y, D, seed = 42) r2 <- function(p, a) 1 - sum((a - p)^2) / sum((a - mean(a))^2)  data.frame(   nuisance    = c("E[Y|X]", "E[D|X]"),   grf_oob_r2  = c(r2(cf$Y.hat, Y),     r2(cf$W.hat, D)),   meep_oof_r2 = c(r2(m$y_hat_oof, Y),  r2(m$d_hat_oof, D)) ) #>  nuisance grf_oob_r2 meep_oof_r2 #>    E[Y|X]      0.854       0.900 #>    E[D|X]      0.865       0.916

what: Linear models via ols() and logreg()
title: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms
text: ols() fits ordinary weighted least squares; logreg() fits binary logistic regression IRLS. C++ engines, classical HC0-HC3 robust standard errors, optional bagging, standard formula matrix interfaces. also slot meep() opt-learners (learners = c("ols", "logreg")).
code: fit <- ols(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars) summary(fit, robust = "HC3")            # HC3 robust standard errors predict(fit, mtcars[1:3, ], interval = "confidence")  df  <- data.frame(am = mtcars$am, mtcars[c("wt", "hp")]) lr  <- logreg(am ~ wt + hp, data = df) predict(lr, df[1:3, ], type = "response")

title: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares
text: Fits Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines (MARS) model (Friedman 1991). forward pass adds pairs hinge basis functions form max(0, +/-(x - knot)); backward pass prunes terms minimising GCV criterion (K-fold CV criterion requested).

title: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares
code: ares(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'formula' ares(x, data = NULL, subset = NULL, ..., y = NULL)  # Default S3 method ares(   x,   y,   degree = 1L,   nk = NULL,   penalty = NULL,   thresh = 0.001,   minspan = 0L,   endspan = 0L,   adjust.endspan = 1L,   auto.linpreds = FALSE,   fast.k = 10L,   fast.beta = 1,   nprune = NULL,   pmethod = c("backward", "none", "cv"),   nfold = 0L,   ncross = 1L,   stratify = TRUE,   seed.cv = NULL,   cv.1se = FALSE,   autotune = FALSE,   autotune.speed = c("balanced", "quality", "fast"),   autotune.warmstart = TRUE,   n.boot = 0L,   na.action = c("impute", "omit"),   family = c("gaussian", "binomial", "poisson", "gamma"),   weights = NULL,   varmod = c("none", "const", "lm"),   trace = 0L,   nthreads = 0L,   ... )

title: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares
text: x numeric matrix data frame predictors, model formula calling formula method. ... Currently ignored. data data frame. Used formula method. subset Optional row-subsetting vector formula method (integer indices logical), passed stats::model.frame(). Ignored default (matrix/data-frame) method. y numeric response vector length nrow(x). family = "binomial", 0/1 numeric, logical, 2-level factor. degree Maximum interaction degree. Default 1 (additive). Use 2 3 two- three-way interactions. nk Maximum number basis terms forward pass. Default scales ncol(x): min(200, max(20, 2 * ncol(x))) + 1. penalty GCV penalty per knot. Default 2 degree = 1, 3 otherwise. Larger values produce sparser fits. thresh Forward-pass early-stop threshold relative RSS improvement. Default 0.001. minspan Minimum gap knots along variable. 0 (default) picks automatic value n p. endspan Distance data ends within knots forbidden. 0 (default) picks automatic value. adjust.endspan Multiplier applied endspan candidate hinge deepen existing interaction. Default 1 gaussian, poisson, gamma; 2 binomial. Pass explicit value override. auto.linpreds TRUE, hinge pairs whose best knot sits variable's range boundary replaced linear term. Default FALSE gaussian, poisson, gamma; TRUE binomial. Pass explicit value override. fast.k Size Fast-MARS candidate cache (Friedman 1993). Larger values rescore candidates step (slower, slightly accurate); 0 rescores every candidate every step. Default 10. fast.beta Age penalty stale entries Fast-MARS cache. Default 1.0. relevant fast.k > 0. nprune Maximum number terms backward pruning. Default nk. pmethod Pruning method. "backward" (default): minimise GCV backward-elimination subsets. "none": keep forward-pass terms. "cv": K-fold cross-validated subset selection. Requires nfold > 0 (sets nfold = 5 specified). nfold Number CV folds pmethod = "cv". Default 0 (CV; GCV-based pruning). nfold > 0, pmethod promoted "cv". ncross Number CV repetitions (builds fresh fold partition; per-size mean MSE averaged). Default 1. stratify TRUE (default), CV folds quantile-stratified y. Useful small n skewed responses. seed.cv Optional integer seed CV fold partition. Pass integer reproducible CV; NULL (default) uses current RNG state. cv.1se TRUE, applies one-standard-error rule pmethod = "cv": among sizes within one SE minimum mean CV-MSE, pick smallest. Default FALSE (pick argmin). autotune TRUE, runs inner cross-validated grid search (degree, penalty, nk, fast.k) refits winner full data. chosen settings full grid scores returned $autotune. Default FALSE. autotune.speed Speed/quality trade-autotune. used autotune = TRUE. "balanced" (default): explores moderate fast.k range picks smallest whose CV-MSE within 1% best. "quality": disables Fast-MARS cache (thorough, slowest). "fast": forces aggressive cache (cheapest, slight accuracy trade-). autotune.warmstart TRUE (default), autotune first tunes small subsample. one cell wins decisively, full grid skipped winner refit rows. applies autotune = TRUE n >= 200. n.boot Number bootstrap replicate fits bagging. Default 0 (bagging). > 0, predict() averages replicates plus central fit (se.fit = TRUE) returns per-prediction bag standard deviation. Composes autotune: replicate reuses central fit's chosen hyperparameters. na.action Strategy missing values x. "impute" (default) replaces column's NAs column's training median stores medians predict() reuse. "omit" drops rows NA. Either action warns. Missing values y, NaN/Inf anywhere x, always rejected. family Response family final coefficient fit. "gaussian" (default): identity link, OLS. "binomial": logit link. y may 0/1 numeric, logical, 2-level factor. "poisson": log link. y must non-negative integer-valued. "gamma": log link. y must strictly positive. non-gaussian families, term selection runs numeric y (fast); selected basis refit response scale via stats::glm.fit() requested family. weights Optional non-negative numeric vector length nrow(x) weighted least-squares fitting. Default NULL (unweighted). Term selection, GCV / CV pruning, autotune respect weights. Negative, NA, NaN weights rejected. varmod Residual variance model used predict() prediction intervals (gaussian ). "none" (default): variance model stored; interval = "pint" unavailable. "const": stores single residual SD; intervals yhat +/- qt(level, df) * sigma. "lm": fits small linear model |resid| yhat allow simple yhat-dependent heteroscedasticity. Captures residual scale changes linearly fitted mean. capture residual scale depends predictor whose contribution yhat small – case |resid| ~ yhat slope close zero "lm" collapses roughly "const". suspect x-driven heteroscedasticity (e.g. variance depends covariate orthogonal mean structure), varmod = "lm" help coverage degrade high-variance regions. Ignored non-gaussian families. trace Trace level. 0 (default) silent; 1 reports forward-pass progress. nthreads Number threads. 0 (default) uses RcppParallel::defaultNumThreads(). Examples vignette cap 2 CRAN compliance.

title: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares
text: object class "ares": list containing fitted coefficients, basis matrix bx, term directions dirs knots cuts, indices selected.terms, training rss gcv, fitted.values residuals, predictor names namesx, call, echoed control parameters. Non-gaussian fits additionally include $family, $glm (deviance, null.deviance, df.null, df.residual, aic, converged, iter), $linear.predictor; $fitted.values response scale (probabilities binomial, positive means poisson gamma). Autotune fits carry $autotune bagged fits carry $boot.

title: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares
text: Two interfaces provided. formula method takes model formula data frame. default method takes numeric predictor matrix x numeric response vector y. Fits deterministic across thread counts: fixed seed.cv, results bit--bit identical regardless nthreads.

title: Kernel Regularized Least Squares — krls
text: Fits Kernel Regularized Least Squares model Gaussian kernel, selecting ridge penalty leave-one-cross-validation via closed-form identity Hainmueller Hazlett (2014).  Marginal effects variances computed default returned original (X, y) scale.

title: Kernel Regularized Least Squares — krls
code: krls(X, ...)  # S3 method for class 'formula' krls(X, data = NULL, subset = NULL, ..., y = NULL)  # Default S3 method krls(   X,   y,   sigma = NULL,   lambda = NULL,   derivative = TRUE,   binary = TRUE,   vcov = TRUE,   weights = NULL,   subset = NULL,   L = NULL,   U = NULL,   tol = NULL,   eigtrunc = NULL,   lambda.method = c("loo", "gcv", "cv", "mll"),   lambda.grid = NULL,   nfold = 0L,   ncross = NULL,   stratify = TRUE,   seed.cv = NULL,   cv.1se = FALSE,   autotune = FALSE,   autotune.grid = NULL,   autotune.nthreads = NULL,   autotune.speed = c("balanced", "quality", "fast"),   autotune.warmstart = TRUE,   varmod = c("none", "const"),   n.boot = 0L,   na.action = c("impute", "omit"),   approx = c("exact", "nystrom"),   nystrom_m = NULL,   landmarks = NULL,   landmark_method = c("random", "kmeans"),   landmark_seed = NULL,   nystrom_eps = 0.000000001,   ard = c("none", "cheap"),   ard.alpha = 1,   ard.cap = 100,   ard.imp = c("avgderiv", "vsq"),   whichkernel = c("gaussian", "linear", "poly1", "poly2", "poly3", "poly4"),   poly_c = 1,   loss = c("ls", "logistic"),   trace = NULL,   nthreads = 0L,   print.level = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'krls_rr' predict(   object,   newdata = NULL,   se.fit = FALSE,   interval = c("none", "pint"),   level = 0.95,   type = c("response", "link", "prob", "variance", "class"),   unscale = FALSE,   ... )

title: Kernel Regularized Least Squares — krls
text: X numeric matrix data frame predictors (n x p). Constant columns rejected. Factor / character columns data frame expanded via model.matrix(~ ., x) (treatment contrasts, intercept dropped). Missing values handled via na.action (see ). ... Currently unused (caught forward compatibility). data Used formula method. data frame containing variables referenced formula. subset Optional integer logical vector restricting rows data / X used fit. Mirrors formula-method behaviour. X supplied directly (matrix data frame), subset slices X, y, weights immediately input validation; NULL (default) -op back-compatible. y numeric response vector single-column matrix.  Constant y rejected. sigma Gaussian-kernel bandwidth.  Default NULL, sets sigma via geomean_p formula: sqrt(median(d2) * p) d2 pairwise squared Euclidean distances standardised predictors p = ncol(X). May supplied strictly positive scalar (since v0.0.0.9045) length-ncol(X) vector per-feature lengthscales (manual ARD): Gaussian kernel becomes K_ij = exp(-sum_k (x_ik - x_jk)^2 / sigma_k). Vector form incompatible autotune = TRUE approx = "nystrom" version; error fit time. per-feature sigma, lengthscales apply column-standardised predictor matrix used internally (value 1 corresponds one unit sd(X[, k])). lambda Optional ridge penalty.  NULL (default), selected golden-section search LOO error. derivative Logical.  TRUE (default), compute pointwise marginal effects average per variable.  Requires vcov. binary Logical.  TRUE (default), columns X exactly two unique values treated binary marginal effects replaced predicted-Y first differences (matches KRLS::fdskrls). vcov Logical.  TRUE (default), compute coefficient covariance variance average marginal effects. weights Optional vector observation weights (length n, strictly positive). Internally normalised mean 1. Implements weighted KRLS via D K D transform D = diag(sqrt(w)). weights = rep(1, n) byte-identical unweighted path. L, U Optional lower / upper bracket lambda search.  NULL, defaults follow KRLS::krls(). tol Tolerance lambda golden section.  Default 1e-6 (fixed, independent n; 1e-3 * n earlier versions). fixed small tolerance gives 6-digit lambda precision regardless sample size. eigtrunc Optional eigenvalue truncation cutoff (0, 1]. set, eigenvalues eigtrunc * max(d) dropped solve.  NULL (default) keeps eigenvalues. lambda.method Lambda-selection rule. "loo" (default) uses closed-form leave-one-golden-section search; "cv" uses K-fold CV grid (nfold > 0 required). "gcv" uses closed-form generalised cross-validation criterion (Craven & Wahba 1979) using eigendecomposition; recommended n large LOO behaves unstably. "mll" (since v0.0.0.9049) selects lambda minimising negative Type-II marginal log- likelihood eigenbasis. Incompatible approx = "nystrom". lambda.grid Optional numeric vector lambda candidates lambda.method = "cv". NULL (default) auto-generates log-spaced grid [L, U]. nfold Number CV folds lambda.method = "cv" autotune. 0 (default) disables CV. ncross Number CV repetitions (builds fresh fold partition). Default NULL, resolves 2 autotune sigma search (repeated-CV stabilisation) 1 lambda.method = 'cv'. Explicit integer values honoured paths. stratify TRUE (default), CV folds quantile- stratified y. seed.cv Optional integer seed CV fold partition. cv.1se TRUE, applies one-standard-error rule picking lambda CV (smallest model within 1 SE minimum mean CV-MSE). Default FALSE. autotune TRUE, runs inner repeated-CV grid search sigma (default: 9-point multiplicative grid centred median-heuristic sigma anchor, anchor * 0.125 anchor * 32) refits winner full data using one-standard-error rule (selects largest sigma within 1 SE minimum CV-MSE). Default FALSE. autotune.grid Optional numeric vector sigma candidates autotune. NULL (default) uses 9-point anchor-centred grid sigma_anchor * c(0.125, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32) sigma_anchor geomean_p anchor (sqrt(median(d2) * p) standardised predictors). autotune.nthreads Integer. Number worker threads used evaluate autotune sigma grid parallel. NULL (default) reads getOption("roadrunner.krls.autotune.nthreads"), falls back 1L unset. Capped length(autotune.grid). used autotune = TRUE. autotune.speed Speed/quality knob autotune dispatch. used autotune = TRUE. One : "fast": scalar sigma grid (v0.0.0.9046 behaviour exactly). "balanced" (default): dispatches cheap-tier ARD ncol(X) >= 20 ncol(X) >= nrow(X) / 10 (heuristic high-p / sparse-signal regimes); otherwise scalar sigma . "quality": always dispatches ARD sweeps 6-cell grid ard.alpha {0.5, 1, 2} x ard.imp {"avgderiv", "vsq"}; picks winner inner CV. low-p dense data "balanced" byte-equivalent "fast". autotune.warmstart TRUE (default), autotune first pre-fits 15% subsample (capped 200 rows) runs one ARD probe; probe improve held-MSE isotropic baseline 2%, ARD dispatch branch dropped autotune collapses scalar sigma . Skipped n < 200 autotune.speed = "fast". varmod Residual variance model used construct prediction intervals via predict(..., interval = "pint"). "none" (default) disables PIs; "const" uses homoscedastic sigma_hat estimated training residuals. n.boot Number bootstrap replicates bagging. 0 (default) disables bagging. n.boot > 0, prediction averages across n.boot replicate fits. na.action handle missing values X. "impute" (default) replaces NAs column median (stored fit reapplied predict() time). "omit" drops rows NA. Missing y always error. approx Either "exact" (default) "nystrom". exact path runs original O(n^3) eigendecomposition full kernel. "nystrom" replaces low-rank Nystrom approximation anchored nystrom_m landmarks, giving O(m^3) + O(n m^2) cost. default nystrom_m = ceiling(sqrt(n) * 3) test RMSE typically within +10% exact smooth DGPs. nystrom_m Integer. Number landmarks approx = "nystrom". Default NULL resolves ceiling(sqrt(n) * 3) (e.g. n=2000 -> m=135, n=5000 -> m=213). Tuned empirically keep test-set RMSE within +10% exact smooth DGPs. Ignored landmarks supplied explicitly. landmarks NULL (default; auto-draw via landmark_method), integer vector row indices X, m x d numeric matrix landmark coordinates original X scale (auto-standardized internally using training centers/scales). landmark_method Either "random" (default; uniform subsample training rows) "kmeans" (Hartigan-Wong centers standardized X; bit-stable across R versions). Ignored landmarks supplied. landmark_seed Optional integer seed local landmark draw. supplied, uses .with_seed() avoid disturbing caller's global RNG state. Required byte-identical fits across consecutive krls(approx="nystrom") calls. nystrom_eps Numeric. Relative ridge floor applied landmark-kernel eigenvalues: D_reg = max(D, nystrom_eps * max(D)). Defaults 1e-9. Stabilizes m x m eigendecomposition landmarks near-collinear; fit object's nystrom_diagnostics$floored_count reports many eigenvalues hit floor (useful tuning m). ard Automatic ARD (per-feature lengthscale) selector. "none" (default) disables ARD selection; supply scalar length-ncol(X) vector sigma manually. "cheap" enables two-pass orchestrator: pass 1 fits isotropic anchor krls() sigma_anchor, derives per-feature importance average marginal effects (row-mean- square gradient, see ard.imp), pass 2 refits s_k = sigma_iso * (median(imp) / imp_k)^ard.alpha clipped [sigma_iso / ard.cap, sigma_iso * ard.cap]. Incompatible approx = "nystrom" user-supplied vector sigma; error fit time. autotune = TRUE user supplies ard = "cheap" explicitly, autotune routes ARD regardless autotune.speed. ard.alpha Mapping exponent importance ratio. 0 reduces isotropic; recommended range [0.5, 2.0]. Default 1.0. ard.cap Symmetric multiplicative ceiling per-feature bandwidth: s_k clipped [sigma_iso / ard.cap, sigma_iso * ard.cap]. Prevents eigendecomposition collapse near-zero importance features. Default 100. ard.imp Importance source cheap-tier mapping. "avgderiv" (default) uses |avgderivatives[k]|; "vsq" uses mean(derivatives[, k]^2), slightly robust signal. whichkernel Kernel choice. "gaussian" (default) preserves full back-compatible Gaussian RBF kernel. "linear" uses inner-product kernel K_ij = x_i' x_j. "poly1", "poly2", "poly3", "poly4" use K_ij = (x_i' x_j + poly_c)^d d = 1..4. Non-Gaussian kernels incompatible approx = "nystrom", ard != "none", vector sigma; error fit time. var.avgderivatives defined non-Gaussian kernels version (returns NA one-shot warning). poly_c Polynomial kernel offset constant. Default 1.0. Used whichkernel starts "poly". loss Loss function. "ls" (default) kernel ridge least squares (path v0.0.0.9049). "logistic" optimises penalised binomial deviance via IRLS coefficient space (since v0.0.0.9050). Requires y strictly {0, 1}. Incompatible approx = "nystrom", lambda.method c("gcv", "mll"), varmod != "none", predict(type = "variance") — error fit / predict time. trace Integer. 0 silent, > 0 enables progress diagnostics (currently: prints chosen lambda > 1, golden-section progress > 2). Replaces print.level. nthreads Integer. Number threads use C++ kernel build / decomposition. 0 (default) means use RcppParallel::defaultNumThreads(). print.level Deprecated alias trace. Prefer trace. object fitted "krls" object. newdata numeric matrix data frame columns training X (matching training formula).  NULL returns object$fitted. se.fit Logical.  TRUE, return pointwise standard errors predictions.  Requires fit created vcov = TRUE. interval Prediction-interval mode. "none" (default) returns point predictions ; "pint" returns lower/upper bounds confidence level level, requires fit created varmod = "const" (non-"none" varmod). level Confidence level interval = "pint". Default 0.95. type Prediction type. "response" (default) returns predictions original y scale. "link" currently synonym "response" (KRLS GLM link; reserved future logistic-loss extension). "prob" valid binary fits (y exactly two unique values) returns plogis(yhat). calibration shortcut: underlying fit still least-squares loss, logistic loss; values clamped [0, 1] true posterior probabilities. "variance" (since v0.0.0.9049) returns GP posterior variance K** - K* (K + lambda )^{-1} K*' per row newdata. supported approx = "nystrom". unscale Logical. used type = "variance". TRUE, multiplies returned variance var(y_train) sits original y scale; FALSE (default), returns standardised-scale variance.

title: Kernel Regularized Least Squares — krls
text: object S3 class c("krls_rr", "krls") components mirroring KRLS::krls(): K, coeffs, Looe, fitted, X, y, sigma, lambda, R2, derivatives, avgderivatives, var.avgderivatives, vcov.c, vcov.fitted, binaryindicator. Formula-method fits additionally carry call, terms, xlevels, factor_info, na.action, na.medians downstream predict(), update(), model.matrix() support. Looe follows KRLS::krls() scale convention: sum squared leave-one-residuals standardised y scale multiplied sd(y), units [y^2 / sd_y] = [y].  preserved downstream compatibility code consumed KRLS::krls() output; LOO MSE raw-y squared units.

title: Kernel Regularized Least Squares — krls
text: Three call styles supported (mirrors ares()): krls(X, y, ...)  – matrix / numeric interface (back-compatible). krls(y ~ x1 + x2 + ..., data = df, ...) – formula interface factor expansion derived terms ((x^2), poly(x, 2), etc). krls(df, y, ...) df data frame numeric + factor / character columns – expands categorical columns via model.matrix(~ ., df) (treatment contrasts, intercept dropped). numerical pipeline mirrors KRLS::krls() exactly: X y standardised (column-centred, unit sd). Gaussian kernel K_ij = exp(-||x_i - x_j||^2 / sigma) built parallel C++. K eigendecomposed.  subsequent solves use eigen-basis closed forms, never inverting K + lambda directly. lambda selected golden-section search closed-form LOO error sum, (L, U, tol) bracket KRLS::krls. Marginal effects use closed-form identity dy/dx_k = -(2/sigma) * (X_k * (K c) - K diag(c) X)_k, computed without forming n x n distance matrix. Output unstandardised back original (X, y) scale. fixed (sigma, lambda), fits agree KRLS::krls() floating-point precision (typically < 1e-12 coefficients, fitted values, marginal effects n <= 1000).  Wall-clock time roughly 6-10x faster KRLS::krls() n >= 500 marginal effects variance estimates requested. Memory scales O(n^2): kernel squared eigenvector matrix stored.  Expect 0.4 * n^2 / 1e6 MB peak working memory (e.g. ~400MB n = 1000, ~10GB n = 5000). Scale-aware sigma default (geomean_p anchor, v0.0.0.9041) sigma = NULL, roadrunner sets Gaussian-kernel bandwidth using geomean_p formula: let d2_ij = ||Xs_i - Xs_j||^2 pairwise squared Euclidean distances standardised predictor matrix Xs; sigma = sqrt(median({d2_ij : < j}) * ncol(Xs)).  geometric mean raw median heuristic p = ncol(X), empirically lands sweet spot two extremes.  n=500, p=10 standardised N(0,1) data yields sigma ~ 13.5, versus ~ 18-19 raw median 10 KRLS::krls()'s fixed default. geomean_p: 15-DGP head--head vs KRLS::krls() (REQ-20260518-003, iter-2) geomean_p anchor shows roadrunner wins 12/15 DGPs, loses 0/15, ties 3/15.  raw median anchor (v0.0.0.9040) won 4/15 DGPs -smoothed locally nonlinear signals (exp-decay, interaction, tanh-interaction, poly2) selecting sigma ~2x wide.  geomean_p anchor preserves scale-awareness (adapts actual data distribution, unlike hard-coded sigma = p) avoiding -smoothing. n > 500 pairwise distance matrix O(n^2); keep default cheap, 500-row subsample drawn fixed seed (set.seed(2718)) result deterministic within session. Autotune-equals-default equivalence well-fit settings autotune enabled settings default sigma already near- optimal (e.g. additive interaction DGPs n=500, p=10), autotune grid centred sigma_anchor CV argmin coincides grid centre.  1-SE rule selects sigma_anchor, producing fit identical non-autotuned default.  expected behaviour — confirms default sigma well-chosen DGPs — failure autotune.  Autotune yields improvements optimal sigma departs anchor (e.g. sparse / linear DGPs much wider kernel better). Since v0.0.0.9042 autotune inner loop parallelised sigma candidates using RcppParallel (TBB). pairwise squared-distance matrix computed per CV fold reused across every sigma grid (exp(-D / sigma) cheap elementwise op). Determinism preserved: worker writes unique slot output vectors, result byte-identical regardless autotune.nthreads. Pass autotune.nthreads = 1 force strictly sequential execution.

title: Kernel Regularized Least Squares — krls
text: fixed (sigma, lambda) fits remain byte-identical earlier versions roadrunner KRLS.  four defaults changed v0.0.0.9040 (sigma, tol, autotune nfold/ncross, autotune grid) affect results arguments left NULL / default.  Restore old default explicitly: sigma = ncol(X), tol = 1e-3 * nrow(X), nfold = 5, ncross = 1, autotune.grid = ncol(X) * c(0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8). sigma anchor formula refined v0.0.0.9041 raw median (median(d2)) geomean_p formula (sqrt(median(d2) * p)).  restore v0.0.0.9040 median anchor, pass sigma = stats::median(.numeric(stats::dist(scale(X)))^2). Phase 1 speedup (v0.0.0.9042): parallel autotune assumes single-threaded BLAS small kernel operations inside parallel region. set high process-global BLAS thread count (e.g. via RhpcBLASctl::blas_set_num_threads(8)), consider resetting 1 around krls(..., autotune = TRUE) calls avoid oversubscription. BLAS-heavy distance computation runs OUTSIDE parallel region benefits multi-threaded BLAS. approx = "nystrom" currently incompatible observation weights user-supplied lambda-search bracket L/U/tol. error clear message fit time. vcov = TRUE supported single-fit Nystrom path resulting vcov m-length dual coefficients, n-length kernel coefficients. Phase 3 may extend . Since v0.0.0.9043 krls() supports opt-Nystrom low-rank approximation via approx = "nystrom". Replacing full n x n eigendecomposition m x m one (m = nystrom_m, default ceiling(sqrt(n) * 3)) gives ~5x speedup n=2000 ~10x n=5000 RMSE typically within +10% exact fit smooth DGPs. Landmarks default uniform random subsample training rows; pass landmark_method = "kmeans" centroidal landmarks. Fits byte-identical fixed landmark_seed seed.cv.

title: Kernel Regularized Least Squares — krls
code: set.seed(1) n <- 100 X <- matrix(rnorm(n * 3), n, 3) colnames(X) <- c("age", "income", "score") y <- sin(X[, 1]) + 0.5 * X[, 2]^2 - 0.3 * X[, 3] +   rnorm(n, sd = 0.2)  fit <- krls(X, y) fit #> Kernel Regularized Least Squares (KRLS) #>   n = 100   p = 3  #>   sigma = 3.813   lambda = 0.222    R^2  = 0.9563  #>  #> Average Marginal Effects: #>        age     income      score  #>  0.6021180 -0.1008827 -0.2742915  fit$avgderivatives           # average marginal effect per variable #>           age     income      score #> [1,] 0.602118 -0.1008827 -0.2742915 summary(fit) #> Kernel Regularized Least Squares (KRLS) #>   n = 100  p = 3  sigma = 3.813  lambda = 0.222  R^2 = 0.9563 #>   Effective df: 24.71 #>  #> Average Marginal Effects: #>         Estimate  Std. Err  t value  Pr(>|t|)     #> age     0.602118  0.022834  26.3690 < 2.2e-16 *** #> income -0.100883  0.021381  -4.7182 7.822e-06 *** #> score  -0.274292  0.020456 -13.4086 < 2.2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>  #> Quartiles of Pointwise Marginal Effects: #>           age     income      score #> 25% 0.3618781 -0.6924617 -0.3714887 #> 50% 0.7342455 -0.2581948 -0.3193604 #> 75% 0.8978449  0.5280357 -0.2050795  ## Formula interface with mixed numeric + factor predictors. df <- data.frame(age = X[, 1], income = X[, 2],                  region = factor(sample(c("N", "S"), n, replace = TRUE)),                  y = y) fit_f <- krls(y ~ age + income + region, data = df)  ## Predictions on new data with pointwise SEs. Xnew <- matrix(rnorm(20 * 3), 20, 3) colnames(Xnew) <- colnames(X) pr <- predict(fit, Xnew, se.fit = TRUE) head(pr$fit) #>             [,1] #> [1,]  1.62457968 #> [2,]  0.01886781 #> [3,] -0.36472318 #> [4,] -0.94094352 #> [5,]  0.09923114 #> [6,]  1.15424173 head(pr$se.fit) #>            [,1] #> [1,] 0.08130310 #> [2,] 0.11459798 #> [3,] 0.07798571 #> [4,] 0.09297983 #> [5,] 0.11479828 #> [6,] 0.09957523

title: Cross-fitted causal ensemble of ares() and krls() — meep

NULL gives outcome-fit (d_hat_oof, arm models). folds Either single integer K (number folds), length-n integer vector fold ids honored verbatim. learners Character subset c("ares", "krls", "ols", "logreg"), named list adapter closures (extensibility hook). default four: c("ares", "krls", "ols", "logreg"). ares krls family-agnostic apply every nuisance. "ols" "logreg" unregularized linear learners hyperparameters (tune -op – plain refit per fold); applied per nuisance family, ols gaussian (continuous) targets logreg binomial (binary) ones. learner apply nuisance skipped, leaving -NA OOF column ensemble excludes; counted fold failure. Narrowing learners names family-incompatible nuisance (example learners = "ols" binary outcome) error. Custom list-learners never family-filtered. ensemble combine learners: "stack" (non-negative least squares OOF matrix), "average" (equal weights), "best" (pick single lowest-OOF-loss learner). arm_models "auto" fits arm-specific outcome models (\(\mu_0\), \(\mu_1\)) treatment binary; "always" forces ; "never" suppresses . family Outcome family, "gaussian" "binomial". NULL auto-detects. treatment_family Treatment family, "gaussian" "binomial". NULL auto-detects. tune One "", "per_fold", "none". See Tuning. cluster Optional length-n grouping vector. Folds assigned cluster level cluster lands entirely within one fold. weights Optional length-n case weights, forwarded every base fit NNLS stacking objective. seed Optional integer seed fold construction. ares_args named list extra arguments spliced every ares() call. krls_args named list extra arguments spliced every krls() call. ols_args named list extra arguments spliced every ols() call (relevant "ols" among learners). logreg_args named list extra arguments spliced every logreg() call (relevant "logreg" among learners). verbose Logical; TRUE, print progress per nuisance / fold. ... text: family NULL, binary y (exactly two distinct values) triggers ares(family = "binomial") krls(loss = "logistic"); otherwise gaussian path used. logic applies treatment_family: binary treatment modelled propensity score via logistic regression, continuous treatment via gaussian regression. treatment three distinct values rejected (multi-valued / continuous-treatment IRM scope). family also governs default learners fitted nuisance. ares krls family-agnostic always apply. ols fitted gaussian (continuous) nuisances logreg binomial (binary) nuisances. Y-model D-model can differ family, applicability resolved per nuisance: gaussian outcome binary treatment fits ols outcome/mu0/mu1 logreg treatment. learner apply nuisance simply skipped – OOF column stays -NA recorded fold failure.

title: Cross-fitted causal ensemble of ares() and krls() — meep
text: "" (default) – autotune learner full data, freeze resulting hyperparameters, refit frozen settings within every fold. Standard DML practice: fit honesty (cross-fitting) protects inference; freezing hyperparameters deliberate, second- order compromise. "per_fold" – autotune independently inside every fold. Purist option; slower. "none" – fast path. Call ares() krls() default arguments (plus anything passed via ares_args / krls_args). autotune invoked .

title: Cross-fitted causal ensemble of ares() and krls() — meep
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(1) n <- 800; p <- 5 X <- matrix(runif(n * p), n, p) m  <- 0.4 * X[, 1] + 0.3 * sin(3 * X[, 2]) D  <- m + rnorm(n, sd = 0.3) g  <- X[, 1]^2 + 0.5 * X[, 3] Y  <- 1.0 * D + g + rnorm(n, sd = 0.5)        # true ATE = 1.0 fit <- meep(X, Y, treatment = D, folds = 5, seed = 42) fit #> meep(): cross-fitted causal ensemble #> Call: meep(X = X, y = Y, treatment = D, folds = 5, seed = 42) #>  #>   n = 800,  p = 5 #>   folds: 5-fold cross-fitting #>   learners: ares, krls, ols, logreg #>   outcome family: gaussian  |  treatment family: gaussian #>   ensemble: stack  |  tune: once #>  #> Ensemble weights: #>   outcome  : ares=0.213  krls=0.066  ols=0.720  logreg=0.000 #>   treatment: ares=0.704  krls=0.296  ols=0.000  logreg=0.000 #>  #> OOF loss (per nuisance, ensemble): #>   outcome  : mse = 0.378 #>   treatment: mse = 0.09565 # Hand the honest OOF nuisances to a causal forest: # grf::causal_forest(X, Y, D, Y.hat = fit$y_hat_oof, W.hat = fit$d_hat_oof) # }

what: roadrunner: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms — roadrunner-package
title: roadrunner: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms — roadrunner-package
text: roadrunner collection fast, low-dependency implementations classical machine learning algorithms thin, base-R-style interfaces. two algorithms shipped today :

title: roadrunner: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms — roadrunner-package
text: ares() – Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines (MARS) GCV / cross-validated pruning, hyperparameter autotune, bagging, weights, binomial / poisson / gamma families, prediction intervals. Built Friedman's (1991) fast least-squares forward selection parallelised knot search. krls() – Kernel Regularized Least Squares (Hainmueller Hazlett 2014) closed-form leave-one-lambda selection, per-observation marginal effects, binary first-difference handling. engines C++ top Rcpp, RcppArmadillo, RcppParallel. Fits deterministic across thread counts fixed seed.

title: roadrunner: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms — roadrunner-package
text: Friedman, J. H. (1991). Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines. Annals Statistics, 19(1):1-67. Hainmueller, J. Hazlett, C. (2014). Kernel Regularized Least Squares: Reducing Misspecification Bias Flexible Interpretable Machine Learning Approach. Political Analysis, 22(2):143-168.

what: Tier A audit bug fixes (8 fixes)
title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9055
text: release closes eight bugs identified 2026-05-22 audit. Full plan: docs/superpowers/audits/2026-05-22-package-audit/01-tier--fix-plan.md. BUG-014 (ares): pmethod = "cv" crashed "subscript bounds" small fold’s forward pass collapsed intercept-model (M_full == 1). C++ engine populates $path.subsets / $path.coefs M > 1, lists empty M=1 case. CV loop now guards index list length scores intercept-prediction directly $coefficients, letting fold contribute valid size-1 MSE. BUG-015 (predict.ares): fit carried $factor_info ($terms), model.matrix() ran model.frame() global na.action (default na.omit), silently dropping newdata rows NA numeric columns. returned vector shorter nrow(newdata), violating predict-length contract. branches now build model frame na.action = na.pass NAs flow downstream median-impute / NA-row-mask path. BUG-016 (krls): predict(weighted_krls_fit, type = "variance") returned ~0 across rows. fit stashed (V, dvals) weighted kernel K_w = D K D (D = diag(sqrt(w))), predict-side helper built K_star unweighted kernel. helper now column-scales K_star sqrt(w_tr) quad-form, implementing V[f*] = K**(unwt) - K* D (K_w + lam )^-1 D K*'. BUG-017 (krls): predict(bagged_krls_fit, newdata = NULL) returned $fitted – central fit – instead bag mean across replicates. non-NULL branch already bagging, NULL-newdata fast-path inconsistent predict--newdata behaviour. fix routes bagged NULL-newdata calls non-NULL branch (newdata = object$X). BUG-018 (meep): predict.meep(nuisance = "mu0" | "mu1") returned -NA weighted meep() fit. arm-restricted refit inside predict.meep passed object$weights (length n) without slicing treatment-arm subset, base learner errored length(weights) != length(y), error swallowed, prediction collapsed NA. Weights now sliced arm rows. BUG-019 (meep): factor character outcome three levels silently coerced integer codes fit regression. Multi-class outcomes scope; meep() now rejects upfront clear message, mirroring treatment-validation pattern. BUG-020 (logreg): null.deviance disagreed stats::glm() -intercept formulas (y ~ 0 + x). C++ engine always used weighted mean y null mu, canonical null model intercept eta = 0 -> mu = 0.5. engine now takes has_intercept flag (R-side detected "(Intercept)" %% colnames(X) intercept = TRUE), uses mu0 = 0.5 false. BUG-021 (plda): nfold > min(table(y)), stratified fold builder placed class folds 1..length(class), folds received zero test observations biased CV error toward 0 (selecting wrong lambda). plda() now rejects upfront clear message recommending largest valid nfold.

Y-model D-model can differ family, applicability resolved per nuisance. gaussian outcome binary treatment fits ols outcome/mu0/mu1 logreg treatment. learner apply nuisance skipped cleanly: OOF column stays -NA (ensemble already excludes -NA columns) recorded fold failure. Narrowing learners names family-incompatible nuisance (example learners = "ols" binary outcome) now error naming nuisance, family, cause. Custom list-learners never family-filtered. changes default meep() output: default OOF prediction matrices now carry ols/logreg columns ensemble weights recomputed larger learner set.

what: ols() and logreg() — unregularized linear fitters
title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9053
text: Two new user-facing functions, ols() logreg(): fast, low-dependency unregularized linear fitters C++ (Rcpp/Armadillo) engines, classical HC robust standard errors, package’s standard base-R-style API. ols(x, y, weights, n.boot, varmod, ...) — ordinary weighted least squares via weighted economy-QR solve. Matrix formula interfaces. Returns S3 object class "ols". rank-deficient design matrix rejected clear error rather silently dropping columns. logreg(x, y, weights, n.boot, ...) — binary logistic regression iteratively reweighted least squares (Fisher scoring). Matrix formula interfaces; accepts 0/1 numeric, logical, two-level-factor response. Returns S3 object class "logreg". Non-convergence (signature quasi-complete separation) reported warning. Robust SEs — classical HC0–HC3 sandwich covariances; surfaced summary() predict() via robust argument. Bagging — n.boot > 0 refits bootstrap-weight resamples; bagged predict() returns bag mean. Serial loop, deterministic across thread counts. Prediction intervals — ols() supports varmod = "const" | "lm" heteroskedastic gaussian prediction intervals (shared ares() variance-model helper). S3 methods — predict, print, summary, plot classes. meep() integration — "ols" "logreg" opt-meep() learners (ols = gaussian, logreg = binomial). default learners stays c("ares", "krls"). hyperparameters, tune -op : every cross-fitting fold plain refit.

title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9052
text: New user-facing function plda(): penalized Fisher’s linear discriminant analysis (Witten & Tibshirani 2011) L1 fused-lasso penalties, multi-class support, built-CV autotune, parallel CV fold execution. plda(x, y, K, lambda, penalty, autotune, ...) — matrix formula interfaces. Returns S3 object class "plda" carrying discriminant matrix, class means, within-class standard deviations, (autotune = TRUE) full CV result. Penalties — penalty = "L1" (default) applies coordinate-wise soft thresholding; penalty = "fused" adds fused-lasso difference penalty (Condat 2013 total-variation prox) controlled lambda2. Multi-class — supports G >= 2 classes; K discriminant vectors G - 1. Autotune — autotune = TRUE (default) cross-validates lambda K log-spaced grid; parallel CV fold loop via TBB roadrunner determinism invariant (byte-identical results nthreads). S3 methods — predict.plda (class, posterior, projection), print.plda, summary.plda, plot.plda (2-D discriminant projection).

what: meep() — Phase Q-DML: cross-fitted causal ensemble
title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9051
text: New user-facing function meep(): pure-R orchestration layer package’s ares() (MARS) krls() (Kernel Regularized Least Squares) fitters produces honest, cross-fitted (--fold) nuisance estimates Double Machine Learning causal-forest workflows. meep() estimate treatment effect — returns cross-fitted predictions residuals, meant handed downstream grf::causal_forest() (via Y.hat / W.hat) DML estimator. meep(X, y, treatment = NULL, ...) — grf-style matrix/vector interface. Returns S3 object class "meep" carrying y_hat_oof, d_hat_oof, mu0_hat_oof/mu1_hat_oof, y_resid, d_resid, folds vector, per-nuisance OOF prediction matrices, ensemble weights, per learner--nuisance OOF performance. Stacking ensemble — ensemble = "stack" fits non-negative least squares honest OOF prediction matrix (leakage already purged column-wise, second nesting layer needed). "average" "best" cheaper fallbacks. NNLS solver hand-rolled (Lawson–Hanson active-set) — new package dependencies. tune = c("", "per_fold", "none") — "" (default) autotunes learner full data, freezes hyperparameters, refits per fold; "per_fold" autotunes inside every fold; "none" fast path calls ares() / krls() defaults never invokes autotune. Family auto-detection — binary outcome routes ares(family = "binomial") + krls(loss = "logistic"); binary treatment modelled propensity score. Multi-valued treatments rejected clear error. Cluster-robust folds — cluster argument keeps whole clusters within fold. Graceful degradation — learner errors fold dropped rows, ensemble weights renormalized survivors per row, event logged $fold_failures. predict.meep(), print.meep(), summary.meep() (overlap diagnostic binary treatments) S3 methods. grf added Suggests gated integration test.

what: krls() — Phase Q5: true logistic-loss IRLS path (B1)
title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9050
text: Closes biggest functional gap vs lukesonnet/krls2: real logistic ridge regression kernel basis, optimised via IRLS penalised binomial deviance. Default loss = "ls" remains byte-identical v0.0.0.9049 (back-compat snapshot test-krls-ard-kernel.R continues pass ULP tolerance, loss = "ls" fits byte-equal v9049 tolerance = 0). loss = c("ls", "logistic") new argument krls.default(). Default "ls" existing kernel ridge LS path. "logistic" optimises minimise -2 sum(y log p + (1-y) log(1-p)) + lambda c' K c p = plogis(K c), via Cholesky-backed Newton step coefficient space (option ii spec): iter solves (K W K + lambda ) c = K W z W = p(1-p) working response z = eta + (y - p) / W. ~5-10 iters typical default tolerance 1e-6; cap 50. Step-halving (5 per iter) penalised-deviance increase. Numerical guards: W floored 1e-8, eta clipped [-30, 30], perfect-separation detection (||c||_inf > 1e6 penalised dev < 1e-10) warns returns last finite iterate. Compose rules. loss = "logistic" requires binary y {0, 1}. Combinations don’t make sense (deferred) error early: + approx = "nystrom" — deferred (large-n use case). + lambda.method = "gcv" — Wahba 2-D extension; deferred. + lambda.method = "mll" — Laplace approx; deferred Q9. + varmod != "none" — residual variance model LS-specific. predict(type = "variance") — Q6 derive IRLS-aware posterior variance; current Q5 errors. Composes fine : whichkernel = "linear"/"poly*", ard = "cheap", autotune = TRUE (deviance scoring; force R fallback Gaussian-specialised C++ inner LS-specific), n.boot > 0 (bagged averaging LINK scale, sigmoid end Jensen), lambda.method = "loo" (closed-form CT-2008), lambda.method = "cv" (fold-deviance scoring), derivative = TRUE (link-scale marginal effects default), weights (case weights baked IRLS W = w * p(1-p)). CT-2008 closed-form LOO logistic. Cawley & Talbot 2008 leave-one-approximation, computed coefficient space single H = K (K W K + lambda )^{-1} K solve. diagonal H_diag stashed fit (one extra n x n solve per converged IRLS). cv_loo_dev replaces Looe logistic. Predict gains type = "class" (hard 0/1 prediction). logistic fits: type = "link" returns eta; type = "response" type = "prob" return calibrated probabilities; type = "class" returns .integer(p > 0.5). binary LS fits, type = "class" uses existing plogis--LS shortcut. Print / summary. Logistic fits print "loss: logistic (IRLS, k iter, converged)" line, use McFadden pseudo-R-squared place LS R-squared, append "(link scale)" suffix average marginal effects header.

New R helpers: .krls_logistic_lambdasearch, .krls_logistic_fit_block, .krls_bag_logistic. .krls_autotune_scalar() accepts is_logistic = FALSE; TRUE, per-cell IRLS fits + deviance scoring replace LS solve. .krls_run_cheap_ard() accepts loss forwards pass-1 isotropic fit ARD logistic works end--end. slim_krls() strips eta_fitted H_diag along existing heavy intermediates. slim_krls(keep_predict = FALSE) retains loss, deviance, converged, iter, cv_loo_dev inspection-grade summary. var.avgderivatives logistic NA (full IRLS-aware derivation deferred Q6) one-shot warning.

what: krls() — Phase Q2: polynomial kernels + GP variance + MLL
title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9049
text: Three bundled feature items implemented behind single polymorphic C++ kernel dispatch. Gaussian default fits byte-identical v0.0.0.9048; non-Gaussian kernels open K_ij = x_i' x_j (linear) K_ij = (x_i' x_j + poly_c)^d d 1..4 (polynomial). A1 — whichkernel = c("gaussian", "linear", "poly1..4") + poly_c. Default "gaussian" unchanged. "linear" inner-product kernel; "poly1..4" inhomogeneous polynomial kernels offset poly_c (default 1.0). Non-Gaussian kernels incompatible approx = "nystrom", ard != "none", vector sigma — error fit time clear messages. Marginal effects computed via closed-form dgemm route (D = d * H * X H = (X X' + c)^(d-1) poly, constant per column linear). var.avgderivatives currently NA non-Gaussian kernels (full derivation deferred Phase Q6); one-shot warning surfaces fit time. Autotune dispatches: Gaussian sweeps sigma (9-pt anchor, existing); poly sweeps poly_c (default c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8)); linear collapses lambda-(errors user supplies autotune.grid). Non- Gaussian autotune runs R fallback scalar autotune helper rather Gaussian-specialised krls_autotune_inner_cpp — expect 5-10x wall-clock regression vs Gaussian autotune matching grid sizes; acceptable Q2 scope. fit list gains $whichkernel, $kernel_type, $poly_c. A6 — predict(..., type = "variance"). New type value returns GP posterior variance K** - K* (K + lambda )^{-1} K*' per row newdata via new krls_posterior_var_cpp helper. Closed-form computation eigen-basis fit’s kernel: reuses cached dvals + V (now stashed fit v9049). New unscale arg (default FALSE): TRUE, multiplies returned variance var(y_train) sits raw y scale. Incompatible approx = "nystrom" (require m-length Phi spectrum — deferred). Bagged fits average per- replicate posterior variances. Matches brute-force Kss - diag(K* (K + lI)^{-1} K*') reference tol = 1e-8 n=50. FP-floored 0. A11 — lambda.method = "mll". Closed-form Type-II marginal log-likelihood lambda-selection objective. Pure-R loss .krls_mll_loss(dvals, Vty, lambda, n) reuses eigen- basis cache LOO/GCV. Incompatible approx = "nystrom". Composes autotune sigma (Gaussian) non-Gaussian whichkernel. Sigma--MLL selection deferred Q8.

title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9049
text: Four small parity items, R-side (C++ changes), batched together avoid four trivial release cycles. A3 — Neffective (effective df). Fits now carry $Neffective, trace smoother hat matrix H = K (K + lambda )^{-1}. Computed eigen-basis sum(d / (d + lambda)), d kernel eigenvalues used solve. Nystrom path uses m-length Phi' Phi spectrum formula generalises sum(Sigma2 / (Sigma2 + lambda)). summary(fit) prints "Effective df: <value>" line near existing R^2 line. Neffective -> 0 lambda -> Inf -> n (rank K) lambda -> 0. A7 — subset krls.default(). matrix / data-frame default method now accepts subset = NULL (-op, back-compatible) logical / integer vector selecting rows X. y weights sliced lockstep downstream processing. Mirrors formula method’s subset semantics. A8 — predict(..., type = "prob") binary fits. predict.krls_rr() gains type = c("response", "link", "prob") argument. "response" (default) preserves prior behaviour byte--byte. "prob" valid fit binary (y exactly two unique values, tracked fit via new $binary_y field) returns plogis(yhat). docstring explicit: calibration shortcut, true posterior probability; underlying fit remains least-squares loss. "link" synonym "response" reserved future logistic-loss extension. A9 — slim_krls() / unslim_krls(). New exported helpers cheap serialisation. slim_krls(fit, keep_predict = TRUE) (default) strips heavy K, V, Vsq, Vty, vcov.c, vcov.fitted intermediates preserving everything predict() needs; saved size typically drops >50% n >= 200. slim_krls(fit, keep_predict = FALSE) strips inspection-grade summary (coeffs, R2, Looe, marginal effects, sigma, lambda, Neffective); predict() errors clear message. unslim_krls() -op placeholder — refit krls() stored $call rebuild dropped fields.

what: krls() — automatic two-pass ARD selector (cheap tier)
title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9046
text: New argument ard = "cheap" enables two-pass automatic ARD pipeline: pass 1 fits isotropic KRLS scale-aware sigma anchor; pass 2 refits per-feature lengthscales derived pass-1 marginal- effect importances via s_k = sigma_iso * (median(imp) / imp_k)^alpha, clipped [sigma_iso / cap, sigma_iso * cap]. New args ard.alpha = 1.0 (mapping exponent), ard.cap = 100 (symmetric multiplicative ceiling), ard.imp = c("avgderiv", "vsq") (importance source). Default ard = "none" byte-identical v0.0.0.9045. Composes bagging (n.boot), CV (lambda.method = "cv"), GCV, weights, varmod. Rejects autotune + ARD Nystrom + ARD fit time (constraints manual vector sigma P2a). Compute cost: ~3-5x scalar isotropic. Pass 1 throwaway fit; varmod / binary / n.boot forced , vcov stays engine couples derivative = TRUE (need importance extraction). pass-1 outputs discarded per-feature importance vector read . Empirical lift: grf::generate_causal_data aw3 n_train=750, p=100 (sparse signal, mostly noise dimensions), test R^2 rises 0.13 (isotropic) 0.33 (ard=‘cheap’), comparable ranger (0.34) data.

what: krls() — scale-aware sigma anchor refined to geomean_p (REQ-20260518-003)
title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9041
text: default sigma anchor formula .krls_sigma_anchor() updated raw median heuristic (median(d2), introduced v0.0.0.9040) geomean_p formula: sqrt(median(d2) * p) d2 pairwise squared Euclidean distances standardised training matrix p = ncol(X). : 15-DGP head--head vs KRLS::krls() n=500, p=10 (iter-0, REQ-20260518-003) showed v0.0.0.9040 median anchor -smoothed locally nonlinear signals, producing 8 losses 0 ties favour KRLS. anchor sweep 6 candidate formulae (iter-1) identified geomean_p clear winner (14/15 DGPs). Verification patched anchor (iter-2) confirmed: roadrunner wins 12/15 DGPs, KRLS wins 0/15, 3 ties. Delta table — iter-0 (median) vs iter-2 (geomean_p) mean MSE ratio (rr / KRLS): n=500, p=10 standardised N(0,1) data geomean_p anchor gives sigma ~ 13.5, splitting difference raw median (~18–19) KRLS’s fixed sigma = p = 10. formula still data-adaptive: non-standardised non-unit-variance inputs differ sensibly extremes. one-line R change (R/krls.R, .krls_sigma_anchor()); C++ engine logic unchanged. fixed (sigma, lambda) fits remain byte-identical prior versions. restore v0.0.0.9040 median anchor, pass sigma = stats::median(.numeric(stats::dist(scale(X)))^2).

title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9040
text: Addresses overfitting confirmed diagnostic sweeps REQ-20260518-001. four changes R-level ; C++ engine unchanged, fixed (sigma, lambda) fits remain byte-identical earlier versions. Empirical improvement signal DGPs (n=500, p=10, tune=none, R=10 replications; overfit ratio = test_MSE / train_MSE): improvement attributable Fix 1 (sigma default) Fix 2 (lambda tolerance); Fixes 3 4 stabilise autotune sigma selection. Breaking defaults (old values restorable explicit arguments): Default sigma changed ncol(X) median pairwise squared Euclidean distance standardised predictors (‘median heuristic’). n=500, p=10 oracle sigma ~20 median heuristic anchors near neighbourhood; sigma = ncol(X) 10 (2x -smoothed). Restore old behaviour sigma = ncol(X). Default lambda tolerance changed 1e-3 * n (n-dependent, coarse moderate n) 1e-6 (fixed, 6-digit precision). LOO golden-section empirically selecting lambda ~4x argmin sigma=20, n=500 old tolerance. L-bracket climb now uses multiplicative steps (x10 per step) instead additive steps (0.05 per step) scale-robustness. Restore old behaviour tol = 1e-3 * nrow(X). Autotune sigma grid changed ncol(X) * c(0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8) (6 points fixed d) sigma_anchor * c(0.125, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32) (9 points centred median heuristic anchor). Restore old behaviour autotune.grid = ncol(X) * c(0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8). Autotune CV stabilised: default folds raised 5 10; default cross-partition repeats raised 1 2 (via ncross argument, whose default changes 1L NULL); sigma selection now applies 1-SE rule (largest sigma within 1 SE minimum CV-MSE, biasing toward wider kernels evidence weak). autotune component fit gains new fields: ncross, mse_per_fold, se_mse, cv.1se, sigma_1se. Restore old fold count nfold = 5; restore old repeat count ncross = 1.

what: New feature: krls() – Kernel Regularized Least Squares
title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9033
text: Adds krls(), -scratch implementation Hainmueller Hazlett (2014) KRLS estimator roadrunner roof. algorithm mirrors KRLS::krls() exactly (standardisation, Gaussian kernel, eigen-basis closed-form solve, golden-section LOO lambda search, marginal effects, binary first-difference handling); matched (sigma, lambda) fits agree KRLS::krls() within floating-point precision (< 1e-12 coefficients, fitted values, pointwise average marginal effects, prediction SEs). Engine C++ (src/krls.cpp) top RcppArmadillo (added LinkingTo + Imports) RcppParallel. Gaussian kernel test-vs-train kernel built parallel TBB worker. Eigendecomposition dispatched LAPACK via arma::eig_sym divide--conquer driver. Marginal effects computed via identity dy/dx_k = -(2/sigma)*(X_k*(K c) - K diag(c) X)_k, avoids explicit n x n distance matrix reduces average- marginal-effect variance O(n^3) O(n^2) per variable via row-sum trick. Measured speed-KRLS::krls() simulated benchmarks (derivative = TRUE, vcov = TRUE) nthreads = default: n=200 p=3 ~2x; n=500 p=3 ~5-6x; n=500 p=10 ~10x; n=1000 p=3 ~6x; n=1000 p=10 ~10x. Coefficient max-abs error vs KRLS::krls() < 2e-13 every cell. S3 class c("krls_rr", "krls") predict(), print(), summary() dispatch unambiguously roadrunner methods even KRLS loaded session. inherits(fit, "krls") preserved downstream-compat checks. Tests: tests/testthat/test-krls.R (parity vs KRLS::krls coefficients, fitted values, Looe, marginal effects, prediction SEs, binary first-differences; structural input-validation; predict recovers fitted values; sensible R^2). 11 tests, 28 assertions, green.

title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9032
text: BUG-013 (usability, low): print.ares() summary.ares() used silent bagging (n.boot > 0) autotune state, bagged autotuned fit printed identically plain fit. Fix: add one-line Bagging: n.boot = N replicate(s) Autotune: degree=D penalty=P nk=K fast.k=F warmstart=T/F blocks printers corresponding components present. summary.ares also carries $boot $autotune print method. Regression test: tests/testthat/test-bug-013-print-bag-autotune.R. BUG-011 (correctness, medium): ares.formula() silently dropped subset = ... (fell ... went nowhere) silently absorbed offset(...) terms ordinary predictors. produced wrong fits zero indication. Fix: () add explicit subset arg ares.formula pass model.frame via lm()-style match.call() construction (NSE inside model.frame doesn’t trip subset symbol resolving base R function); (b) detect offset() terms via attr(terms, "offset") model.frame runs stop() actionable message. Offset pass-post-hoc GLM refit punted later release (touches predict + bag + autotune compose paths). Regression test: tests/testthat/test-bug-011-formula-subset-offset.R. BUG-008 (correctness, high): predict() used return finite WRONG values newdata rows containing NA training used na.action = "omit". pre-existing warning promised “affected rows return NA predictions” code never imposed NA – NaN > 0 C++ hinge evaluates FALSE, collapsing affected hinge 0 yielding deterministic wrong prediction. Fix: detect NA rows xnew C++ basis pass, zero-fill NaN cells, re-impose NA rows linear-predictor compute (including bag mean, bag SE, interval = "pint" matrix path). Regression test: tests/testthat/test-bug-008-predict-na-rows.R. BUG-009 (correctness, high): bagged predict(..., type = "link") non-gaussian families used return g(mean(g^{-1}(eta_b))), .e. link applied response-scale bag mean. Jensen’s inequality mean(eta_b), simulator audit showed divergences hundreds log-odds units binomial bags moderate signal – making type = "link" numerically unreliable downstream use. Fix: collect per-replicate linear predictors etas response-scale predictions resps separately; type = "link" returns rowMeans(etas), type = "response" returns rowMeans(resps) (unchanged prior behaviour). Bag SE computed whichever scale returned. Regression test: tests/testthat/test-bug-009-bagged-link-jensen.R. BUG-012 (correctness, medium): formula-path fits derived terms ((x^2), poly(x, 2), log(x + 10), scale(x), splines::bs(x), …) used fit successfully predict() fail “newdata missing columns: (x^2)” predict.ares looked expanded column name literal column newdata, re-evaluating original terms object newdata. Fix: object$terms non-null (formula path), use model.matrix(delete.response(terms), newdata, xlev = object$xlevels) rebuild design derived terms re-evaluated. Falls back prior column-lookup path terms object stored (matrix interface). ares.formula now also stashes xlevels fit. Regression test: tests/testthat/test-bug-012-predict-derived-terms.R. BUG-010 (robustness, medium): sister BUG-004. NA values factor / character newdata columns used fall OOV detector (handled non-NA character values), model.matrix(~ ., newdata)’s default na.action = na.omit silently dropped rows – length(predict(fit, newdata)) less nrow(newdata). Fix: detect NA factor / character newdata columns front; error clear message naming column(s), mirroring BUG-004’s OOV path. Regression test: tests/testthat/test-bug-010-predict-factor-na.R.

title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9031

Measured speedup v0.26 speed baseline (inst/sims/v0.26-speed-baseline.R, 24-cell grid x 5 reps, nthreads=4): geometric-mean 1.23x across grid; autotune cells specifically 1.05-1.15x (gaussian highdim p=20: 1.09x; gamma n=1000 p=10: 1.11x; binomial n=1500 p=20: 1.15x). Determinism invariant (nthreads=1 == nthreads=N) preserved. Internal C++ entries gain optional compute_forward_qr (mars_fit_cpp) R_in / Qty_in (mars_backward_only_cpp) parameters; public R API unchanged.
